diagrams.title: Types of Polymorphism in C++
diagrams.description: A high-level overview showing the two main categories of polymorphism in C++: Compile-time (Static) Polymorphism and Runtime (Dynamic) Polymorphism, with their respective subtypes
diagrams.purpose: Provide a conceptual map of all polymorphism types available in C++ and how they are categorized

diagrams.title: Function Overloading Example
diagrams.description: Demonstrates compile-time polymorphism through function overloading, showing multiple functions with the same name but different parameter lists (signature) within the same scope
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate how C++ achieves compile-time polymorphism through function overloading and parameter differentiation

diagrams.title: Runtime Polymorphism with Virtual Functions
diagrams.description: Shows the mechanism of runtime polymorphism using a base class pointer referring to different derived class objects, demonstrating how the correct function is called at runtime based on the actual object type
diagrams.purpose: Explain how virtual functions enable dynamic method dispatch and runtime polymorphism in C++ through inheritance hierarchies

diagrams.title: Inheritance Hierarchy for Polymorphism
diagrams.description: Displays a typical class inheritance diagram with a base class 'Shape' and derived classes 'Circle', 'Rectangle', and 'Triangle', each potentially overriding a virtual 'draw()' function
diagrams.purpose: Visualize a real-world example of polymorphism using shapes with a common interface and implementation-specific derived classes

diagrams.title: Virtual Table (Vtable) Mechanism
diagrams.description: Illustrates the internal implementation of virtual functions showing how each object with virtual functions contains a vptr (virtual table pointer) that points to a vtable containing function pointers for the class
diagrams.purpose: Explain the underlying mechanism (vtable/vptr) that enables runtime polymorphism in C++ and how function dispatch works internally
